title: trichera arvensis subsp. carpathica
intro: Trichera arvensis subsp. carpathica, also known as Carpathian thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it beneficial for various health conditions. The health benefits of Carpathian thyme include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and congestion, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Carpathian thyme include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, boost the immune system, and support overall well-being.
